metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the 'What' section to explicitly cover the three additional compact.py call sites that read `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` (the `_compact_locked` parameter at :165, the `compact_cli` config read at :583, and the `fsck_repair_rebuild` fallback at :440-445) so the plan accounts for the full edit surface.
findings.finding: The plan says to 'Hardcode never-emit in _snapshot_strip_keys (compact.py:127-136): always add "signature" to the dropped-keys set; delete the emit_legacy_mirror branch' — but the emit_legacy_mirror parameter and config-read exist at three additional sites not named in the plan: (a) the _compact_locked function signature at compact.py:165 (parameter with default), (b) compact_cli config read at compact.py:583 (_cfg.emit_legacy_signature_mirror), and (c) the fsck_repair_rebuild fallback load at compact.py:440-445. All three must be updated when the field is removed or the code will reference a non-existent attribute and break at runtime.
findings.location: What section — 'Hardcode never-emit in _snapshot_strip_keys (compact.py:127-136)'; also compact.py:165, :440-445, :583
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Also remove the `emit_legacy_mirror` parameter from `_compact_locked` (compact.py:165), delete the `_cfg.emit_legacy_signature_mirror` read in `compact_cli` (compact.py:583), and remove the config-fallback load in `fsck_repair_rebuild` (compact.py:440-445), replacing all three with the hardcoded `False` / direct `_snapshot_strip_keys(False)` call.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `compact_cli` (compact.py:583) and `_compact_locked` (compact.py:165, 632) as edit targets — remove the `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` read and the `emit_legacy_mirror` parameter from the call chain.
findings.finding: `compact_cli` at `compact.py:583` reads `_cfg.emit_legacy_signature_mirror` from the typed config and passes it as a positional argument to `_compact_locked` at line 632. The plan does not enumerate this read site or the `emit_legacy_mirror: bool = False` parameter on `_compact_locked` (line 165) as edit targets.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the What section: 'Remove the `emit_legacy_mirror` parameter from `_compact_locked` (line 165) and its read in `compact_cli` (line 583); the `_compact_locked` call at line 632 no longer passes it.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log` (compact.py:400–445) to the 'What' cleanup list: remove its `emit_legacy_mirror` parameter, delete the `try/except` config-load block (lines 440–445), and replace the `_snapshot_strip_keys(emit_legacy_mirror)` call with the now-unconditional `_snapshot_strip_keys()` (or inlined constant set).
findings.finding: The plan's 'What' section targets `_snapshot_strip_keys` and the main compact-command path for `emit_legacy_mirror` removal, but does not list `rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log` (compact.py:400–445) as a cleanup site. That function carries its own `emit_legacy_mirror: bool | None = None` parameter and a `try/except` block that reads `config.load_config(_root).compact.emit_legacy_signature_mirror`. After the field is removed from `CompactConfig`, the attribute access silently falls to `except` (AttributeError → `emit_legacy_mirror = False`), which is correct but leaves confusing dead code — and would hard-fail if `REBAR_CONFIG_UNKNOWN_KEYS=error` causes the config load to raise `ConfigError` before the attribute access.
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'What' section, add a bullet: 'Remove the `emit_legacy_mirror: bool | None = None` parameter from `rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log` and delete its `try/except` config-load block (compact.py:440-445); call `_snapshot_strip_keys()` unconditionally at line 446 (mirroring the same change at line 253).'
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out procedure to the Scope or AC section (e.g., 'to recover, revert commit <SHA> / this PR; the lever is restored at the reverted state') so responders have a named recovery path if the gate assessment proves incorrect post-merge.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges the one-way-door nature of this removal and states 'after removal the only recovery is a code downgrade, not a config flip,' but never names an explicit back-out procedure. T4 requires an explicit rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract sequence — acknowledgement of irreversibility alone is insufficient. If the readiness-gate assessment proves wrong (e.g., a pre-attestations consumer appears post-merge), there is no documented recovery path.
findings.location: Scope — 'the one-way door' section; AC item 3
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief 'Back-out plan' subsection under Scope: 'If a pre-attestations reader is discovered post-merge, revert this commit/PR to restore the emit_legacy_signature_mirror lever; then re-assess before re-landing.' Also add a matching AC: '[ ] Back-out procedure documented in release notes or migrations.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate all files/docs that reference `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` outside `migrations.md` (e.g., README, configuration reference, CHANGELOG) and either fix them in this story or create a blocking dependency on the doc-sweep story landing before/with this one.
findings.finding: The plan defers removal of all stale references to `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` (in docs, README, configuration guides, etc.) to a separate 'doc-sweep story' without enumerating those references or requiring the doc-sweep to land atomically with this story. Since this story executes the one-way-door retirement, any surviving references to the field as a live rollback lever become immediately misleading the moment this story merges.
findings.location: What section: 'Doc-sweep coordination' paragraph; AC item 4
findings.suggested_fix: Add a grep/ripgrep scan (`rg 'emit_legacy_signature_mirror'`) as a required step in this story's implementation checklist, and either fix every hit here or gate merge on the doc-sweep PR being co-merged. List the known locations explicitly in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief rollback runbook to `docs/migrations.md` specifying: (1) the git ref/tag to revert to, (2) that redeployment is required before any pre-attestations reader is brought online, and (3) that affected stores must be re-compacted with the reverted binary to restore the `signature` mirror — so that if the readiness-gate assumption breaks post-deploy, MTTR is bounded.
findings.finding: The plan states the only rollback after landing is a 'code downgrade' but does not specify what that means operationally — which ref/tag to revert to, how quickly it can be executed, and critically whether snapshots already compacted without the mirror need a re-compact pass (which requires the old binary) to restore compatibility for any pre-attestations reader that comes back online.
findings.location: Scope — the one-way door section; AC line 3 (operator-attested readiness gate)
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Legacy signature-mirror retirement' section of `docs/migrations.md`, add a 'Emergency rollback' subsection: 'If a pre-attestations reader is discovered after this change lands: (1) `git revert <sha-of-this-commit>` and cut a patch release; (2) deploy the patch; (3) for each affected store run `rebar compact` with the reverted binary to re-emit the signature mirror; (4) bring the pre-attestations reader online only after step 3 completes.' This makes the rollback explicit, bounded, and testable in staging.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the parser's behavior when `rebar.toml` still contains `emit_legacy_signature_mirror` after removal (e.g., emit a deprecation warning and ignore the unknown key, or confirm the parser already silently drops unknown keys) so upgrade is non-breaking for operators with existing configs.
findings.finding: The plan does not specify how the config parser should behave when an existing `rebar.toml` still contains `emit_legacy_signature_mirror = true` (or `false`) after the field is removed from the schema. If the parser enforces strict/no-unknown-keys validation, any operator with the key present will get a hard parse error on upgrade — the plan's one-way-door warning does not cover this immediate breakage path. If the parser silently ignores unknown keys, that is the safe path, but it is unspecified.
findings.location: AC line 1 ('removed from schema, parser, and `rebar.toml` (if present)'); `_config_schema.py` schema/parser sections
findings.suggested_fix: If the parser has a strict-unknown-keys mode, add a one-release deprecation shim that logs a warning and ignores the key before removing the parser entry. If the parser already silently ignores unknown keys, add a sentence to the plan confirming this so implementors do not add a validator that would error.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify any existing tests for the `emit_legacy_signature_mirror=true` branch and either remove them or convert them into a positive assertion that `signature` is never present in compacted snapshots after this change.
findings.finding: The plan deletes the `emit_legacy_mirror` branch in `compact.py` but does not mention locating, updating, or removing any existing tests that exercised the legacy-emit (`emit_legacy_signature_mirror=true`) code path. If such tests exist, they either become dead coverage or silently pass against a stub, providing false confidence; if they fail, the build breaks unexpectedly.
findings.location: AC line: 'make lint && make typecheck && make test pass' / What section: 'Hardcode never-emit in _snapshot_strip_keys'
findings.suggested_fix: Add an AC: 'Any test that configured emit_legacy_signature_mirror=True is removed; at least one test asserts that compacted snapshots never contain the `signature` key regardless of prior config state.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `compact.py:rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log` (lines 440-446) as an explicit edit target — remove the config read and the `emit_legacy_mirror` parameter pass, hardcoding the strip-keys call to always drop `signature`; verify fsck callers in `fsck.py:205` and `fsck.py:447` require no signature change.
findings.finding: `rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log` (compact.py:440-446) is an unenumerated edit site: it reads `config.compact.emit_legacy_signature_mirror` from the config and passes it to `_snapshot_strip_keys`. It is called from `fsck.py:205` and `fsck.py:447`. The plan names only `compact.py:127-136` as the hardcode site and does not name this second config-read or the fsck callers as requiring updates.
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, extend the hardcode bullet: 'Also update `rebuild_snapshot_from_full_log` (compact.py:440-446): remove the `emit_legacy_mirror is None` config-read branch and hardcode `_strip = _snapshot_strip_keys()` (no argument).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name `tests/unit/test_legacy_signature_mirror_drop.py` (rollback-branch tests `test_strip_keys_keep_mirror_on_rollback`, `test_rollback_snapshot_keeps_mirror`) and `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_attestations_integration.py::test_compaction_keeps_mirror_on_rollback` as deletion targets in the What section.
findings.finding: `tests/unit/test_legacy_signature_mirror_drop.py` directly imports and calls `_snapshot_strip_keys(emit_legacy_mirror=True)` and `_snapshot_strip_keys(emit_legacy_mirror=False)` — four call sites testing the rollback-branch behavior being deleted. `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_attestations_integration.py:108-121` (`test_compaction_keeps_mirror_on_rollback`) also exercises the env var `REBAR_COMPACT_EMIT_LEGACY_SIGNATURE_MIRROR=true`. Neither test file is named as a deletion/update target.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to What: 'Delete the rollback-branch tests in `test_legacy_signature_mirror_drop.py` (lines 42-44, 90-95) and `test_compaction_keeps_mirror_on_rollback` in `test_attestations_integration.py` (lines 108-121); retain the drop-by-default and round-trip tests.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC4 / `docs/migrations.md` update, explicitly state the post-rollback schema state for tickets already compacted mirror-less: assert whether raw SIGNATURE events survive compaction (making re-derivation possible after code downgrade), or acknowledge that those tickets are permanently mirror-less and document the recovery path (repair script, re-attestation, or 'accept no signature on those tickets').
findings.finding: After this story lands, the config-flip rollback path (`emit_legacy_signature_mirror = true`) is removed. For tickets whose snapshots were already compacted mirror-less, a subsequent code downgrade + re-compact may not restore the mirror — the raw SIGNATURE events that previously fed the mirror have already been retired into the snapshot during the migration compaction. The plan does not assert whether those raw events are still present in the ticket directory or describe the post-rollback schema state for already-migrated tickets.
findings.location: Scope — the one-way door section; docs/migrations.md lines 158–171 (Rollback block to be updated by AC4)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-section to the migrations.md retirement note: 'Post-rollback state of already-migrated tickets: raw SIGNATURE events are archived as `.retired` files and remain in the ticket directory; a code downgrade to ≤0.7.x + `rebar compact <id>` will re-derive the mirror from those retired events. Confirm this against the compaction retirement policy before shipping.' If retired events are NOT preserved (e.g. a deep-gc purges them), note that explicitly and provide a repair script path.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a proving command to AC1 that produces a compacted snapshot and asserts `"signature"` is absent: e.g. `jq -e '"signature" | IN(keys[])' <compacted-snapshot.json> && exit 1 || echo OK`, or add/cite a specific test that exercises the `_snapshot_strip_keys` hardcode path and asserts the key is not in the output.
findings.finding: AC1's behavioral claim 'new snapshots never persist the `signature` mirror' has no proving command that compacts a snapshot and structurally asserts `signature` is absent from the output artifact; `make test` (AC5) passes if existing tests simply don't cover the absence of this key after the hardcode change.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: '…new snapshots never persist the `signature` mirror.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'Verified by: `python -m pytest tests/compact/test_strip_keys.py::test_signature_never_persisted -v` (a new or existing test that compacts a fixture snapshot and asserts `"signature" not in output_snapshot`).' If no such targeted test exists, add one as part of this story.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the AC or What section an explicit note to retire the `REBAR_COMPACT_EMIT_LEGACY_SIGNATURE_MIRROR` env-var reference in docs/migrations.md:166 and to delete or repurpose the rollback integration test at test_attestations_integration.py:108-121 that exercises it.
findings.finding: The plan does not address the env-var surface for this config key. The auto-derived env name `REBAR_COMPACT_EMIT_LEGACY_SIGNATURE_MIRROR` (per `_canonical_env_name` formula: REBAR_{SECTION}_{KEY}) is documented as a rollback mechanism in docs/migrations.md:166 and is actively exercised by an integration test at tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_attestations_integration.py:113 via `monkeypatch.setenv("REBAR_COMPACT_EMIT_LEGACY_SIGNATURE_MIRROR", "true")`. The plan's doc-sweep mention covers `docs/migrations.md` but does not call out retiring the env var documentation or the test that uses it.
findings.location: What section — doc-sweep coordination paragraph; docs/migrations.md:166; tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_attestations_integration.py:108-121

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
